rulings.Case Title: CAROL BLUMENFELD SACHAL VS. JUAN DE DIOS RODRIGUEZ-PINA BLANCO et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Defendants Notice Of Motion And Motion For Partition Accounting
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Friday, February 10, 2017, line 1. DEFENDANT JUAN RODRIGUEZ-PINA BLANCO's Motion For Partition Accounting Hearing Required to address why the referee should not address the accounting issue and present a recommendation to the Court. Pages 16 to 27 of Opposition is stricken. = (501/REQ)

rulings.Case Title: YUYUN HUANG VS. RESIDENTIAL RENT STABILIZATION AND ARBITRATION ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Hearing On Petition For Writ Of Administrative Mandamus [Ccp 1094.5]
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Friday, February 10, 2017, line 2. PETITIONER YUYUN HUANG's Petition For Writ Of Administrative Mandamus [Ccp 1094.5] DENIED. The Rent Board did not exceed its jurisdiction and did not abuse its discretion in determining that a tenancy was created under the San Francisco Rent Ordinance. The Rent Board did not make any determinations as to the validity of the lease and this issue is not properly before the Court on the writ proceedings. = (501/REQ)

rulings.Case Title: PAUL SALIBA VS. NAJIB EID SALIBA ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Friday, February 10, 2017, line 6. CROSS DEFENDANT NAJIB EID SALIBA AS CO-TRUSTEE OF THE SALIBA, NAJIB SALIBA's DEMURRER TO CROSS COMPLAINT OVERRULED. Cross-complaint sufficiently alleges a cause of action for indemnity for one of the alleged misrepresentations (re: down-payment), which is incorporated by reference in all causes of action in Defendants' Third Amended Cross-Complaint and forms the basis for their claimed damages. Whether Plaintiff will be found liable for intentional tort is not an issue that can be addressed at this stage of the litigation. The Court assumes that language appearing at page 3 on lines 5 and 6 of the Cross-Complaint is a typographical error "breaching his duties to Paul and Najib" and Cross-Complaint shall have 10 days from notice of entry of this order to file and serve an amendment to complaint to correct the error. Defendants shall have 10 days thereafter to file an answer. = (501/REQ)

rulings.Case Title: YAMEN ELTAWIL VS. KIRANSINH BALWANTSINH THAKOR ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Plaintiff Yamen Eltawil'S Notice Of Motion And Motion For Leave To File First Amended Complaint
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Friday, February 10, 2017, line 11. Plaintiff Yamen Eltawil'S Motion For Leave To File First Amended Complaint GRANTED in part and DENIED in part. Motion is granted re: new defendant, prayer for relief and cause of action for constructive eviction. Motion is denied re: addition of a new Plaintiff, a dissolved corporation. First Amended Complaint to be filed and served forthwith. = (501/REQ)

rulings.Case Title: 626 JACKSON STREET LLC VS. PAUL NATHAN ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION TO QUASH SERVICE OF SUMMONS OR STAY OR DISMISS
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for Friday, February 10, 2017, line 10. DEFENDANT THE GREAT STAR THEATER, INC.'s MOTION TO QUASH SERVICE OF SUMMONS OR STAY OR DISMISS GRANTED, subject to opposition. Mr. Nathan was not notified in any way, orally or in writing, that he was being served on behalf of the corporation. Defendants and defendant's counsel are ordered to comply with SFLR 2.7B in future. Failure to do so may result in denial of the motion. = (501/REQ)
